skilled.metaTitle: Canada skilled immigration overview | IRCC planning | MinRosh
skilled.metaDescription: Skilled pathways for Canada: Express Entry concepts, work permits, and PNPs at a high level. Always verify on Canada.ca / IRCC.
skilled.headline: Canada skilled immigration planning
skilled.intro: Canada’s economic immigration includes Express Entry-managed programs, provincial nominations, and various work permits. Eligibility depends on age, language, education, work experience, and sometimes a job offer or provincial criteria. Use IRCC’s official tools and program instructions; MinRosh helps you organise questions and sequencing before you invest in language tests or credential assessments.

sections.title: Where clients often start
sections.body: Language scores, educational credential assessment (where applicable), and a clear work history narrative are common foundations. We help you see how those pieces connect to the program you are considering.
bullets: Provincial program fit versus federal tracks

faq.question: Is Express Entry the same as permanent residence?
faq.answer: Express Entry is a system used to manage applications for certain economic PR programs. Entering the pool is not the same as receiving an invitation to apply.

partner.metaDescription: Family sponsorship and partner routes for Canada at a planning level. Confirm eligibility on IRCC.
partner.headline: Canada partner and family routes
partner.intro: Sponsorship programs have sponsor eligibility, undertaking obligations, and detailed forms. Requirements vary by inland versus overseas routes and by who is sponsored. Read IRCC’s family sponsorship pages for authoritative steps.

sections.title: Planning sponsorship
sections.body: We help sponsors and applicants understand evidence expectations and timelines at a high level before you commit to a complex paper trail.

student.metaDescription: Study permit guidance for Canada: designated learning institutions, funds, and genuine student factors. Official rules on Canada.ca.
student.headline: Study in Canada — study permits
student.intro: Most international students need a study permit tied to a designated learning institution acceptance. You must show you can pay tuition and living costs and that you will leave Canada when your permit ends unless you obtain another status. Check IRCC for the latest study permit checklist.

sections.title: Institution and province
sections.body: DLI choice, province, and post-graduation work permit rules can interact. We help you ask the right questions early.

education.metaTitle: Canada education consultation | DLI & permit alignment | MinRosh
education.metaDescription: Course and institution planning for Canada with study permit awareness.
education.headline: Education planning for Canada
education.intro: Selecting a programme and DLI affects your study permit narrative and costs. MinRosh helps align education choices with realistic visa documentation.

sections.title: Shortlisting
sections.body: We compare options for credibility, duration, total cost, and how they support your stated study goals.

updates.metaTitle: Canada immigration updates | IRCC changes | MinRosh
updates.metaDescription: Tracking Canadian immigration updates via IRCC. MinRosh helps interpret impact on your case.
updates.headline: Canada immigration updates
updates.intro: IRCC publishes news releases, intake pauses, and instruction updates. Treat social media summaries as secondary to Canada.ca. We help clients understand whether a change touches their category.

sections.title: Reliable monitoring
sections.body: Follow IRCC program pages you depend on, and note effective dates when rules change mid-year.

faq.metaDescription: Introductory FAQ for Canada-bound clients working with MinRosh from Australia or abroad.
faq.headline: Canada immigration questions
faq.intro: Answers here are high-level. Use IRCC for binding eligibility and document lists.

faq.question: Do I need biometrics for Canada?
faq.answer: Many applicants must give biometrics. IRCC explains who needs them and how to book.

faq.question: Can MinRosh help with Express Entry points?
faq.answer: We can help you understand factor breakdowns conceptually and what to verify next; precise scoring is determined by IRCC rules in force when you apply.

about.metaDescription: How MinRosh supports Canada visa and education planning alongside Australian services.
about.headline: MinRosh and Canadian migration
about.intro: We help clients compare timing and effort across destinations. For Canada, we emphasise official IRCC guidance and realistic preparation for language tests and document-heavy applications.

sections.title: Our role
sections.body: Structured triage, sequencing, and clarity—not a substitute for reading IRCC instructions where you self-represent.

contact.metaDescription: Contact MinRosh Migration about Canadian study, work, or permanent residence planning.
contact.headline: Contact us about Canada
contact.intro: Share your current country of residence, goal (study, work, PR), and any language test or job offer status. We will suggest next steps and reading on Canada.ca.

sections.title: Ways to connect
sections.body: Use /contact for the form or book a consultation. Mention Canada in the subject or first line.
